image-compressor.name: Image Compressor
image-compressor.intro_focus: Reducing image weight before uploading keeps your email render-time fast and prevents Gmail clipping at 102 KB of HTML.
workflow: Compress your image with the free in-browser tool below (uses MozJPEG + Squoosh-style optimization, no upload)
Download the compressed file (typically 60-80% smaller than the original)
Upload to {ESP_NAME}'s image library or media manager
Insert into your campaign — render speed and total email weight both improve
key_facts_template: {ESP_NAME}'s practical image-size budget per email: under {limit} KB total to avoid Gmail's 102 KB HTML clipping
Recommended max image width: {width}px (matches the standard email body width)
JPEG at quality 80 typically reduces size 60-80% vs the original lossless format
{ESP_NAME} supported image formats: {formats}

gif-compressor.name: GIF Compressor
gif-compressor.intro_focus: Animated GIFs are deliverability-fragile — large GIFs trigger spam filters and slow render times. Pre-compression with gifsicle's lossy presets cuts GIF weight 40-70% without visible quality loss.
workflow: Upload your GIF to the free browser-based gifsicle compressor below (runs entirely client-side via WebAssembly)
Choose a preset: Outlook-safe (sub-500 KB), mobile-friendly (sub-300 KB), or maximum compression
Download the optimized GIF
Upload to {ESP_NAME} — the result animates in Apple Mail, Gmail, Yahoo, and Outlook web, with a static first frame in Outlook desktop
key_facts_template: {ESP_NAME}'s GIF guidance: keep total animated GIF weight under 1 MB for cross-client compatibility
Outlook desktop (Windows 2007–2019) renders only the FIRST frame of animated GIFs
gifsicle's lossy compression at level 80 typically reduces GIF size 40-70% with minimal perceptual loss
Recommended max GIF size for email: 500 KB to stay under the 1 MB combined payload budget

css-inliner.name: CSS Inliner
css-inliner.intro_focus: Outlook desktop (Word rendering engine) ignores <style> blocks entirely. Inlining CSS as style attributes is required for cross-client compatibility, and the free browser-based inliner below does it instantly.
workflow: Paste your HTML email source with <style> blocks into the free CSS inliner below
The tool walks the CSS AST, computes effective styles per element including specificity and !important, and emits style attributes
@media queries, @keyframes, and pseudo-class rules remain in the head <style> block (they cannot be inlined)
Copy the inlined output and paste into {ESP_NAME}'s custom-HTML editor
key_facts_template: Outlook desktop ignores <style> blocks 100% of the time — every critical style must be inlined
CSS specificity is preserved: inline (1000) wins over class (10) wins over element (1)
@media queries cannot be inlined and must remain in <style> blocks for responsive emails
Gmail strips classes that match certain ad-detection patterns, making external CSS unreliable

inbox-preview.name: Inbox Preview
inbox-preview.intro_focus: {ESP_NAME}'s built-in preview shows one rendering — usually their own webmail. Real cross-client testing means simulating Gmail Web, Apple Mail iOS, and Outlook desktop side-by-side before you send.
workflow: Paste your HTML email source into the free inbox preview tool below
See three side-by-side renderings: Gmail Web, Apple Mail iOS, and a strict-mode Outlook approximation
Identify cross-client failures (flexbox layouts that collapse in Outlook, custom fonts that fall back, dark-mode contrast issues)
Iterate before sending through {ESP_NAME}
key_facts_template: Apple Mail (iOS + macOS) handles ~52% of all email opens globally
Outlook desktop on Windows accounts for ~6% globally but >20% in B2B
Gmail caches images on Google's CDN for 7 days, breaking open-tracking pixels
47% of email opens occur on mobile (Litmus 2024 State of Email)

spam-checker.name: Spam Score Checker
spam-checker.intro_focus: Pre-send spam-trigger analysis catches issues before they impact your sender reputation. Free rule-based scoring combined with a Naive Bayes classifier identifies precisely which content triggers fire.
workflow: Paste your campaign HTML into the free spam score checker below
60+ rules evaluate subject patterns, body keywords, header completeness, link density, HTML structure, image-to-text ratio, and authentication signals
A Naive Bayes classifier trained on 50,000+ labeled messages provides a complementary spam-probability score
Review the rule-by-rule breakdown and fix triggers before sending via {ESP_NAME}
key_facts_template: SpamAssassin's default spam threshold is 5.0 — scores above this typically land in spam folders
Naive Bayes classifiers achieve 95-98% accuracy on labeled spam corpora
Subject lines using all caps trigger a +2.0 score penalty in SpamAssassin
Missing List-Unsubscribe header correlates with Gmail spam folder placement
